title: 1. Structure and Function of the Skin
text:
The skin is the largest and heaviest organ in the body. It is the ﬁrst line of defense of  the
immune system. The thickest skin is located at the soles of our feet, and the thinnest is
between the eyebrow and the eye. Thick or thin, the skin protects the feeble body from
gravels, shred of glasses, and even small dust. It is also an insulation device maintaining our
body temperature, and a sensory input device just like our eyes and ears. If our skin is not
performing  its  sensory  function  properly,  we  cannot  lift  a  small  empty  glass  without
breaking it. The skin is divided into three layers. The ﬁrst layer is the epidermis. It is the
outer tissue of the dermis and works as a protective ﬁrst barrier. The second layer is the
dermis, which literally means skin, which provides structure and support. The last layer is
the hypodermis, which is located right under the dermis. The adipose cell (fat) is a major
component that provides insulation and cushioning for the body.
[ Three layers of the skin ]
• Epidermis
• Dermis
• Hypodermis (Subcutaneous layer)
